summary: The main claim is: Auditing advanced AI systems for misalignment and dangerous capabilities — across system evaluations, training-design, deployment, security, and governance audits — is a strongly positive pathway for reducing catastrophic risk.
Audits have real limits (false confidence, safety-washing, overfitting, no behavioral guarantees) and only improve safety if their recommendations are backed by regulatory authority.
